3.2 Computation
To utilize the parallel processing capability of the computer, we used the
’pathos’ python library. In the code segment shown in Listing 1, two main
functions to construct the distance matrix can be seen.
create distance matrix utilizes pooling and further accepts a variable
for selecting the compression algorithm. distance calc function is used to
calculate the normalized compression distance (see Equation 1) for a given
pair of training and test sample.
The experiments are performed on a computer with M1 chip-set and 8
cores. APythonnotebookandaCondaenvironmentwereusedforsimplicity,
portability and reproducibility. The notebook and the Conda environment
configuration files are shared in our repository Ozan [2023].
Running the code as separate Python scripts is faster than executing the
same scripts within Python notebooks.

Nevertheless, the experimental setup
provides insights into the running times of different compression algorithms.
In Table 3 the computation times for each selected compression algorithms
can be seen.
1 def distance_calc(compression, test, train, i, j):
2 test_string = test.iloc[i][‘sequence’]
3 train_string = train.iloc[j][‘sequence’]
4 if compression in [‘gzip’, ‘snappy’, ‘brotli’, ‘bz2’]:
5 compressor = globals().get(compression)
6 Cx1 = len(compressor.compress(test_string))
7 Cx2 = len(compressor.compress(train_string))
8 Cx1x2 = len(compressor.compress((" ".join([test_string, train_string]))))
9 elif compression == ‘lz4’:
10 Cx1 = len(lz4.frame.compress(test_string))
11 Cx2 = len(lz4.frame.compress(train_string))
12 Cx1x2 = len(lz4.frame.compress((" ".join([test_string, train_string]))))
13 elif compression == ‘zstandard’:
14 Cx1 = len(zstd_compress(test_string.encode()))
15 Cx2 = len(zstd_compress(train_string.encode()))
16 Cx1x2 = len(zstd_compress((" ".join([test_string, train_string]))))
17 elif compression == ‘lzma’:
18 Cx1 = len(lzma.compress(test_string.encode()))
19 Cx2 = len(lzma.compress(train_string.encode()))
20 Cx1x2 = len(lzma.compress((" ".join([test_string, train_string]))))
21 distance = (Cx1x2 - min(Cx1, Cx2)) / max(Cx1, Cx2)
22 return [i, j, distance]
23
24 def create_distance_matrix(train, test, pool, compression=‘gzip’):
25 distance_matrix = np.zeros((len(test), len(train)))
26 for i in range(len(test)):
27 result = pool.map(partial(distance_calc, compression, test, train, i), range(len(train)))
28 for j in result:
29 distance_matrix[j[0]][j[1]] = j[2]
30 return distance_matrix
Listing 1: Python functions to construct the distance matrix. Elements of
the matrix corresponds to a normalized compression distance between the
samples from the training set and the test set.
